Title: Four teens arrested for assaulting boy, dunking head in bucket
Article:
MELAKA: Police have detained four secondary school students for allegedly bullying a 12-year-old boy at an orphanage in Pantai Puteri, Tanjung Kling.
Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said the victim was reportedly kicked and punched by a group of senior students.
The suspects also allegedly submerged the victim's head in a bucket of water and dragged him across a toilet floor.
The incident came to light after the boy's mother lodged a police report on Wednesday night after learning of the assault.
Acting on the report, police arrested four boys, aged 15 and 16, the following day.
"The suspects have no criminal records and tested negative for drugs," Dzulkhairi said in a statement.
The suspects were released on police bail after their statements were recorded.
The case is being investigated under Section 507B of the Penal Code for bullying, which carries a maximum penalty of three years' imprisonment, a fine, or both, upon conviction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two arrested after police chase ends in crash in Subang Jaya [WATCH]
Article:
PETALING JAYA: Two people were arrested after a police chase involving a Proton Preve ended in a crash with a BMW at a traffic light junction in Subang Jaya last night.
The incident has gone viral on social media with clips showing the Proton being chased by multiple police cars.
The incident even got the attention of Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh, who was on her way home when the crash occurred.
Yeoh took a video of the crashed BMW and Proton as police secured the scene at the junction.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Shamsudin Mamat said police received a report on the incident at 9.05pm on May 1.
"The chase began along the New Klang Valley Expressway near the Subang toll exit heading towards Shah Alam after the suspects allegedly tried to evade a police inspection.
"The suspect drove dangerously into the Subang Jaya intersection before crashing into a black BMW at the traffic light junction along Persiaran Tujuan, Subang Jaya," he said in a statement today.
He said the suspects were arrested by police at the scene.
"Upon further checks, police found 17 packets containing a substance believed to be drugs weighing about 16g.
"The first suspect, a local man, has a record of 21 criminal and drug-related offences, while the second suspect, a foreign woman, has no priors," he said.
Shamsudin said the man tested positive for methamphetamine while the woman tested negative for drugs.
"Both suspects have been remanded for three days until May 4.
"The case is being investigated under Section 186 of the Penal Code, Section 12(2) of the Dangerous Drugs Act and Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act," he said.
Witnesses can contact the nearest police station or the Petaling Jaya police operations room at 03-7966 2222.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump says US Navy acting 'like pirates' to carry out naval blockade of Iranian ports
Article:
WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump said on Friday the US Navy was acting "like pirates" in carrying out Washington's naval blockade of Iranian ports during the US and Israel's war against Iran.
Trump made the comments while describing the seizure by US forces of a ship a few days ago.
"We took over the ship, we took over the cargo, we took over the oil. It's a very profitable business," Trump said in remarks on Friday evening. "We're like pirates. We're sort of like pirates but we are not playing games."
Some of Teheran's vessels have been seized by the US after leaving Iranian ports, along with sanctioned container ships and Iranian tankers in Asian waters.
Iran has blocked nearly all ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz apart from its own since the start of the war. Trump has imposed a separate blockade of Iranian ports.
The US and Israel attacked Iran on February 28. Iran responded with its own strikes on Israel and Gulf states that host US bases. US-Israeli strikes on Iran and Israeli attacks in Lebanon have killed thousands and displaced millions.
The war has raised oil prices and led to the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, a chokepoint for about 20 per cent of global oil and ​liquefied natural gas shipments.
Trump, who has offered shifting timelines and goals for the war that remains unpopular in the US, has faced widespread condemnation over his comments on the conflict, including when he threatened to destroy Iran's entire civilisation last month.
Many US experts said last month that American strikes on Iran may amount to war crimes after Trump threatened to target civilian infrastructure.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Woman, 62, claims loan sharks assaulted her in restaurant over debt
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A 62-year-old woman has alleged she was assaulted by loan sharks inside a restaurant owned by them over unpaid debts.
During a press conference held alongside the Malaysian Humanitarian Organisation (MHO), the victim, who wishes to be known as Ms Wong, said she had taken out loans to cover medical expenses for her husband, who is battling cancer.
She later claimed she was deceived into signing documents to repay the debt, which resulted in multiple companies and bank accounts being opened under her name, China Press reported.
Although more than a month has passed since the incident, Wong said she still experiences pain in the areas where she was attacked.
Recounting the ordeal, she said that inside a restaurant operated by the alleged loan shark, a young man pulled down the shutters before he and his associates began punching and kicking her.
Her face became severely swollen, and after she collapsed, she was repeatedly kicked in the chest.
"My husband was there at the time, but the assailants restrained him. He could only stand by helplessly, unable to intervene," she said.
She added that the ordeal lasted about two hours before she was finally released. By then, she was too badly injured to walk and had to be carried out by her husband and the loan shark's associates.
"While I was being beaten, I endured the pain without crying. It was only after I was carried out of the restaurant that I broke down in tears.
"I truly do not understand how these young people could be so cruel to an elderly woman," she added.
She has since lodged a police report and provided the authorities with details of the suspects.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] No backing down: 14 Negri Sembilan Umno reps firm on withdrawal of support for MB
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Negri Sembilan Umno chief Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias said the 14 assemblymen from the party who withdrew confidence from Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun will not be reversing their position.
"I have already stated the position of the 14 assemblymen in Negri Sembilan: that we have lost confidence in the leadership of the Menteri Besar of Negri Sembilan.
"This means we will not be shifting our position on this matter," Jalaluddin told reporters on the sidelines of Umno's 80th anniversary celebrations here today.
Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said on Thursday that
all 14 assemblymen had agreed to continue supporting the unity government
in the state.
Zahid said the decision was reached after discussions with the assemblymen.
On whether Umno would support a new Menteri Besar candidate who is also from Pakatan Harapan (PH), Jalaluddin said that decision was not for Umno Negri Sembilan to make.
"That decision rests with the highest leadership, between the Barisan Nasional and Pakatan Harapan leaderships," he said, adding that the party would await whatever the top leadership decided on the matter.
However, Jalaluddin, who is also Jelebu Member of Parliament and Pertang assemblyman, highlighted that the identity of any replacement was secondary to the principle at stake.
"The issue is not about politics, not about power, not about any individual.
"The issue is that a fundamental duty as Menteri Besar is this: you cannot allow a crisis to escalate to the point where an adat crisis erupts in Negri Sembilan," he said.
Asked whether Umno was prepared to sit in opposition if Aminuddin remained in office, Jalaluddin said yes.
"Umno is prepared to become the opposition in government until the next state election or the 16th General Election," he said.
Jalaluddin said the move by the Umno assemblymen was not driven by political ambition or factional manoeuvring.
"This is not a position driven by political interests, by the pursuit of power, or by what people call 'tebuk atap', this is about the governance of a government that is responsible for managing the institution of adat and the Rulers in Negri Sembilan," he said.
He said that the crisis had its roots in mismanagement by the Menteri Besar, which had allowed a constitutional and adat crisis to erupt, one that dragged the royal institution into public controversy.
Jalaluddin warned that the public airing of the adat dispute had already done damage, with social media debates generating conflicting perceptions among the public, particularly among young Malaysians who lacked grounding in the customary traditions of the royal state.
On Tuesday, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the
Menteri Besar would continue serving following a decree by the Yang di-Pertuan Besar Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir
.
Anwar said the state's political crisis was being resolved through legal channels. He said Aminuddin had accepted the royal decree and would continue carrying out his duties while unresolved constitutional matters were clarified.
On April 27, Negri Sembilan Umno and BN withdrew support for the state government led by Aminuddin, citing a crisis involving the state's Adat Perpatih customary system and its constitutional framework.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US sanctions are 'collective punishment,' says Cuba during May 1 marches
Article:
HAVANA: Cuba said Friday that US President Donald Trump's fresh sanctions on the island amounted to "collective punishment," as an enormous May 1 procession outside the American embassy in Havana vowed to "defend the homeland."
Trump has mused about taking over Cuba, which lies 145 kilometres from Florida and has been under a nearly continuous US trade embargo since Fidel Castro led a communist revolution in 1959.
Late Friday, he told an audience in Florida that the US would be "taking over" the Caribbean island "almost immediately."
In an executive order on Friday, the US leader said he would
impose sanctions
on people involved in sweeping sections of the Cuban economy, which is steered by the government.
The latest sanctions constitute "collective punishment" of the nation's people, Cuban Foreign Minister Bruno Rodriguez said.
"We firmly reject the recent unilateral coercive measures adopted by the #UnitedStates government," he posted on X in English. "These actions demonstrate an intention to impose, once again, collective punishment on the Cuban people."
In a Spanish-language statement, he called the sanctions "illegal" and "abusive."
Trump's order said the measures target people known to "operate in or have operated in the energy, defence and related materiel, metals and mining, financial services, or security sector of the Cuban economy, or any other sector of the Cuban economy", as well as Cuban officials judged to have engaged in "serious human rights abuses" or corruption.
Already in the throes of economic stagnation, the situation worsened for Cuba following a fuel blockade imposed by Washington in January, with only one Russian oil tanker making it through since then.
Supply shortages and power cuts have become the norm, and tourism – once Cuba's most lucrative industry – has plummeted.
The sanctions come despite moves toward dialogue between the two countries, with senior US officials visiting the island for talks in April.
Friday's fresh measures took effect during May 1 celebrations, which saw huge crowds in Havana march to the US embassy under the slogan "Defend the Homeland." The march was led by Cuban President Miguel Diaz-Canel and former revolutionary leader Raul Castro.
The day before, Diaz-Canel had called on Cubans to mobilise "against the genocidal blockade and the crude imperial threats to our country," referring to US actions and rhetoric.
During the rally, authorities claimed to have collected more than six million signatures from Cubans "for the homeland and for peace" over the past six weeks.
Opponents, however, have questioned the method by which the signatures were collected.
State television showed thousands more people assembling in other Cuban cities.  - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two new rabies cases detected in Sarawak
Article:
KUCHING: Two new rabies-positive cases have been recorded in Kuching, bringing the cumulative total to 14 cases detected in Sarawak this year.
The latest cases were identified through 41 samples of domestic animals tested between April 27 and May 1.
The Sarawak Veterinary Services Department (JPVS), in a statement on Facebook, said one case was detected in Sungai Tengah Kolong 2 involving a free-roaming pet dog.
Another case was detected in Kampung Sungai Atas Singai, Bau, involving a dog bite incident involving a free-roaming pet dog.
The department said free-roaming domestic dogs are at risk of contracting rabies and may transmit the disease to other animals, posing a threat to human safety.
As such, dog owners are advised to ensure their pets are vaccinated regularly and not allowed to roam freely.
JPVS veterinary officer Dr Ainul Riza Abu Seman said the department continuously conducts free vaccination programmes to encourage more pet owners to vaccinate their animals.
"A dog vaccination programme helps break the chain of infection, which is why we conduct monthly programmes in various local authority areas," she said when met at the Anti-Rabies Vaccination, Licensing and Microchipping Programme at Sungai Maong Market here today.
Dr Ainul said public awareness has been increasing, reflected in the growing number of pet owners bringing their animals for vaccination at JPVS programmes.
"Increased awareness and responsibility can save lives and help achieve a rabies-free community," she said.
For record, since rabies was declared an outbreak in July 2017, a total of 93 human infection cases have been recorded in Sarawak, with 86 fatalities.
Only seven patients survived, most of whom suffered neurological complications.
As of April this year alone, three deaths have been recorded in Sarawak.
END/-
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Canada approves new generic Semaglutide
Article:
OTTAWA, (Canada): Health Canada on Friday authorised a second generic version of the popular diabetes drug semaglutide, further solidifying the country's position as the first G7 nation to approve alternatives to the brand-name drug Ozempic, reported Xinhua.
According to a news release from the federal health agency, the newly approved injection from Canadian-based pharmaceutical company Apotex is indicated for the once-weekly treatment of adult patients with type 2 diabetes to manage blood sugar levels.
The authorisation followed a "thorough review" demonstrating the drug meets Health Canada's criteria for safety, efficacy and quality, said the release.
The move came just three days after Health Canada became the first regulator in a G7 country to approve a generic semaglutide filed by India's Dr. Reddy's Laboratories.
Health Canada said it is currently reviewing seven other submissions from different companies and expects to make more regulatory decisions "in the coming weeks and months."
The availability of generic drugs is expected to have a positive impact in Canada, including potential cost savings for patients and the healthcare system.
In Canada, many generic medications are 45 to 90 per cent cheaper than the brand-name versions, said the release. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Trump is an idiot': Californians fume over soaring petrol prices
Article:
LOS ANGELES: At a gas station in Los Angeles, Ryder Thomas wore a grimace of barely suppressed anger as he filled his pickup truck, watching the cost tick up to US$130 for a full tank – US$30 more than he was paying before the US and Israel attacked Iran.
"I'm mad about the price, but I'm even madder about why it's so high," the 28-year-old told AFP.
This week, pump prices in the United States climbed to their highest level since early 2022, when Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine triggered a global surge in the cost of oil.
Hostilities in the Middle East, which began when Israel and the United States started bombing Iran on February 28, have crimped supply, sending the price of crude surging.
The war launched by President Donald Trump is unpopular all over the United States, with polls showing a clear majority disapprove of a conflict whose aims they think are unclear.
"There was absolutely no need for this war. It's just like when we invaded Iraq, there were no weapons of mass destruction," Thomas said.
"Trump is an idiot, that's all it is."
For weeks, the Republican billionaire has insisted that attacking Iran was necessary to prevent the country from acquiring nuclear weapons, and has repeatedly promised a short conflict.
But despite the overwhelming might of the US military, the Islamic republic has blocked the Strait of Hormuz – a global trade artery used to transport a fifth of the world's oil and gas.
DOMINO EFFECT
The result of this squeeze on supply is rising prices, sending gas in already-expensive California above US$6 a gallon (US$1.59 per liter), compared with around US$4.50 a gallon before the war.
Thomas worries about the domino effect this oil crunch will have on everything else he buys, as the rising cost of transport pushes up the prices of everyday goods like food and clothing.
"I hope the middle-of-the-road voters who got Trump elected start thinking about it and realize what he's done to them," he said of a president who campaigned on reducing costs for everyday Americans.
Trump voter David Chavez, who was waiting among dozens of cars lining up to refuel at Costco – a wholesale retailer whose prices are often a little lower than average – said it was not that simple.
"It is not nice; we don't want to pay more for gas," acknowledged the cameraman as he got his credit card ready to fill up his minivan.
However, "we don't know everything that happens behind closed doors. It's too easy to blame one person for all the problems."
FOOD BANK
Chavez said he voted for Democrats in the past, but switched to Trump because of former president Joe Biden's "poor handling of immigration and poor handling of the economy."
He accused oil giants of taking advantage of the current climate to artificially inflate prices, and pointed out that stricter environmental rules in California amp up the cost to motorists in the massive state.
Nonetheless, he admitted to being disappointed in Trump, but said no one is perfect.
"Trump still handles things a little bit better," he said.
In the same gas line, 73-year-old Flo, who did not want to give her full name, said she no longer knew what or who to believe on the reasons for rising gas prices.
What she knew for certain was that increased fuel costs were making her life much harder.
"With gas this high, I go to the food bank now, more so than ever," the retiree told AFP.
"I've had to cut back on driving too."
Between her pension and a part-time job she takes home around US$3,000 a month – almost half of which is swallowed by rent.
"Life was already hard," she sighed. "But now it's just harder." - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Poll: 61 pct of citizens say US conflict with Iran a mistake
Article:
WASHINGTON: A majority of United States (US) citizens believe their country's military use of force against Iran was a mistake, according to a new poll published on Friday, reported Xinhua.
Sixty-one per cent of US citizens say that the use of military force against Iran was a mistake, with fewer than two in ten believing that the US actions in Iran have been successful, according to the
Washington Post-ABC News-Ipsos
poll.
About four in ten say it has been unsuccessful, while another four in ten say it is "too soon to tell," according to the poll.
The conflict in Iran is "as unpopular among Americans as the Iraq War during the year of peak violence in 2006 and the Vietnam War in the early 1970s,"
The Washington Post
said.
The poll also noted that support for the conflict among Republicans remains high: 79 per cent say it was the right decision.
In addition, most US citizens believe that the military action against Iran carries potential risks.
Among them, 61 per cent think it would increase the risk of terrorism targeting US citizens, 60 per cent believe it would raise the likelihood of the US economy falling into recession, and 56 per cent say the actions increase the risk of weakening relationships with US allies. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MBSP orders closure of nightclub in Bertam
Article:
KEPALA BATAS: The Seberang Perai City Council (MBSP) has ordered a premises operating as a nightclub in Bandar Setia Utara Fontaines, Bertam here to be closed for failing to comply with licensing regulations.
Mayor Datuk Baderul Amin Abdul Hamid said the action was taken following monitoring and inspections conducted at the premises on April 23 and 28.
"Inspections found that the premises failed to comply with several requirements, including selling liquor without a licence, displaying advertisements without a permit and placing additional tables and chairs without approval.
"In addition, a further inspection by MBSP on April 29 found that the business premises had unauthorised structures without our approval," he said when contacted by Bernama today.
He said MBSP has instructed the premises owner to submit a show-cause letter within seven days regarding the matter.
Baderul Amin said that if the owner fails to respond within the stipulated period or if the explanation given is deemed unreasonable, the premises and advertisement licences will be revoked by MBSP based on the offences committed.
Earlier, the issue went viral on social media following public complaints objecting to the nightclub's operations, alleging that it remained open until the early hours of the morning and was located next to a tahfiz learning centre, a female tahfiz hostel and an elderly care centre.
Following that, members of the public together with the Umno Youth Movement of the Kepala Batas division held a peaceful gathering in front of the nightclub, calling for its full closure due to its late-night operations.
They also claimed to have received complaints that patrons were found intoxicated and that liquor bottles were discarded indiscriminately in the area.
— BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Student stabbed 61 times: Jealousy motive behind murder, say cops [WATCH]
Article:
KOTA BARU: Police believe jealousy was the motive behind the killing of a 19-year-old college student whose body was found with 61 stab wounds in Ketereh on Thursday night.
Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said investigators suspect the victim was stabbed to death by her 19-year-old boyfriend after an argument.
Investigations into the case are ongoing, he added.
He said the boyfriend, who was the main suspect, was detained yesterday.
"Initial investigations showed that an argument broke out between the victim and the suspect, which led to her death.
"She is believed to have been stabbed while inside the suspect's car," he told the New Straits Times.
Mohd Yusoff said another youth detained in the case was also a friend of the victim.
"Two others — a 66-year-old man and a 60-year-old woman — are the suspect's parents.
"They were detained to assist in the investigation," he said.
Two youths, suspects in the murder of a college student who was stabbed 61 times, have been
remanded
for seven days.
Two other suspects, a 60-year-old woman and a 66-year-old man, were remanded for three days.
Earlier reports said the four suspects were
arrested
in raids at two separate locations in Taman Murni and Kampung Belukar Luas, Ketereh, between 4pm yesterday and early this morning.
The suspects have no prior criminal records and tested negative for drugs.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Another foreigner falls to death at KLIA Terminal 2
Article:
SEPANG: A 30-year-old foreign man died after falling from the third floor of Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Terminal 2, just hours after a woman from China fell to her death near the same location.
The incidents have gone viral on social media.
KLIA police chief Assistant Commissioner Ravi Munusamy said the incident occurred about 9.10pm last night at the terminal's Level 3 outdoor balcony area.
The Algerian man was seen removing his backpack near a safety barrier.
"He then fell from the location. A medical team that arrived at the scene administered emergency treatment before he was rushed to Hospital Cyberjaya for further treatment," he said in a statement.
Ravi said the victim was pronounced dead at 2.55am today while receiving treatment.
He added that the case is being investigated as sudden death, with the cause of the incident still under investigation.
Those with information can contact the KLIA police headquarters at 03-8776 8222 to assist in investigations.
Earlier that day, a
woman from China
fell to her death at Terminal 2 near the same spot after behaving erratically at the terminal.
The incident occurred around 2.20pm at the Green Zone, Level 3, where she was seen sitting outside a balcony area, crying and acting aggressively.
Security personnel attempted to calm her down, but she became increasingly agitated and resisted efforts to assist her.
In an attempt to prevent further harm, a security officer tried to approach her, but she struggled and subsequently fell into the loading bay area.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Brazil's Bolsonaro hospitalised for shoulder surgery
Article:
BUENOS AIRES: Former Brazilian president Jair Bolsonaro has been readmitted to a hospital in the capital Brasília to undergo shoulder surgery, according to news portal
G1
on Friday, reported German Press Agency (dpa).
The 71-year-old former leader, who is currently serving a prison sentence of more than 27 years for an attempted coup d'état, received special authorisation from the Supreme Federal Court for the transfer.
Bolsonaro is presently serving his term under house arrest due to ongoing health complications.
While Bolsonaro has faced a series of medical issues stemming from a stabbing during 2018 election campaign, officials stated the current procedure is related to a fall in January during which he injured his shoulder.
He was previously hospitalised with pneumonia in March and spent time in intensive care.
Bolsonaro has been in custody since November of last year following a conviction for conspiring with others to orchestrate a coup against the government of his successor, Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva.
But the duration of his incarceration could soon be reduced.
On Thursday, the Brazilian Congress overrode a veto by President Lula, upholding a law that allows for the recalculation of his sentence. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Johor still misses Najib,': Ex-PM still valued by Umno members, says Onn Hafiz
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Johor Umno chief Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has reaffirmed the state chapter's support for Datuk Seri Najib Razak, saying the former prime minister remains a valued figure among party members.
Addressing delegates at Umno's 80th anniversary celebration at the World Trade Centre today, Onn Hafiz said Johor has not forgotten Najib's contributions, particularly during the party's most challenging periods.
"We in Johor miss him. May he be granted strength and calm in facing this great trial," he said.
Onn Hafiz said Johor Umno backs party president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi's efforts to seek justice for Najib.
Najib is currently serving his sentence at Kajang Prison after being convicted of misappropriating RM42 million belonging to SRC International Sdn Bhd.
He was initially sentenced to 12 years in jail and fined RM210 million, but the 16th Yang di-Pertuan Agong later reduced both the jail term and the fine.
Separately, Onn Hafiz stressed the importance of restoring political confidence among younger Malaysians, many of whom associate the field with conflict, slander and division.
He said it was incumbent on leaders to demonstrate that politics could unite and strengthen society.
"Politics, when guided by wisdom and responsibility, can truly benefit the people and the nation," he said.
Reflecting on Umno's history, Onn Hafiz pointed to its role in shaping Malaysia's development through the establishment of institutions such as Felda, Mara and Petronas.
However, he conceded that the party's defeat in the 2018 general election served as a wake-up call, admitting that past shortcomings had eroded public trust.
"That trust cannot be demanded... it must be earned," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tok Mat: Elections are a war of data, not posters
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno deputy president Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan has warned the party against clinging to outdated electoral tactics, saying that winning the upcoming elections require data, flexibility, and unity — not rallies and poster wars.
Speaking at Umno's 80th anniversary celebrations here today, Mohamad, popularly known as Tok Mat, said the party must brace for the Melaka and Johor state elections as well as the 16th general election, all expected in the near term.
"Today's elections are not merely about rallies. They are about analysis.
"Today's elections are not merely a war of posters. They are a war of data," he said in his opening speech.
Mohamad said Umno would not be locking in its electoral strategy prematurely, arguing that fixing an approach too early was a liability rather than an asset.
"Umno's electoral strategy is not determined today. Our strategy is determined by the realities on the ground when the time comes.
"This is not a weakness. This is a strength," he said.
He said the party would instead read public sentiment, assess the strength of its opponents, and adapt its approach accordingly.
"In modern politics, flexibility is a weapon. Our goal is singular: to restore Umno as the dominant party," he said.
Mohamad was equally blunt on the question of internal cohesion, saying a divided party would be defeated before a single vote was cast.
"Victory does not come on its own, brothers and sisters. It is the product of discipline. It is the product of strategy.
"It is the product of unity. If we are divided, we will lose before we have even begun to fight," he said.
On candidate selection, he set out a clear standard: integrity, electability, and proximity to the people, adding that the three criteria were non-negotiable.
The Melaka state assembly is due to expire in December 2026, with the election widely expected to be held before the end of the year.
Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh has confirmed the polls will be held this year, in line with the scheduled end of the current administration's term.
In the last Melaka state election in November 2021, Barisan Nasional secured a landslide victory, winning 21 of the 28 state seats.
Meanwhile, the Johor state assembly must hold its election by June 20, 2027.
In the 2022 Johor state election, Barisan Nasional won 40 of the 56 seats, with Pakatan Harapan securing 12 and Perikatan Nasional winning three.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Search for girl swept away by drain currents enters second day
Article:
KAJANG: The search and rescue (SAR) operation for a
9-year-old girl
feared drowned after being swept away by strong currents in Bandar Mahkota Cheras entered its second day today.
The search has been expanded to include the underground sewerage system around the scene.
Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director (operations) Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the operation, which began at 8am, covers a 5km radius.
"One team will move from the rubbish trap back to the scene, while another team will search the sewerage route from the point where the victim fell," he said at the scene.
He said firefighters will enter the sewerage tunnel for nearly 300m towards Sungai Long.
Ahmad Mukhlis said the underground route is challenging, reaching depths of up to 15m with a diameter of about 2m.
He said the department is working with Indah Water Konsortium to use specialised pole cameras to inspect hard-to-reach areas.
Ahmad Mukhlis said the victim was playing with a ball at her apartment compound with three friends when it fell into a drain.
"The victim is believed to have climbed into the drain to retrieve the ball. We were informed that her friend managed to grab her hand but lost their grip due to the strong currents," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US bypasses congressional review for military sales of $8.6bil to Middle East allies
Article:
WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump's administration has ​bypassed congressional review to approve military sales totaling over US$8.6 billion to Middle Eastern allies Israel, Qatar, Kuwait and the United Arab Emirates. The State Department announcements on Friday came as the US and Israel's war against Iran marked nine weeks since its start and more than three weeks since a fragile ceasefire came into effect. The State Department said US Secretary of State Marco Rubio determined that an emergency existed that required immediate sales to those countries and waived the congressional review requirements for the sales.
The announcements included approving military sales to Qatar of Patriot air and missile defence replenishment services costing US$4.01 billion and of Advanced Precision Kill Weapon Systems (APKWS) costing US$992.4 million.
They also included approval of the sale to Kuwait of an integrated battle command system costing US$2.5 billion and to Israel of Advanced Precision Kill Weapon Systems costing US$992.4 million. The State Department approved a sale to the UAE of APKWS for US$147.6 million.
The US and Israel attacked Iran on Feb 28. Iran responded with its own strikes on Israel and Gulf states that host US bases. US-Israeli strikes on Iran and Israeli attacks in Lebanon have killed thousands and displaced millions.
The principal contractor in the APKWS sales to Qatar, Israel and the UAE was BAE Systems, the State Department said.
RTX and Lockheed Martin were the principal contractors in the integrated battle command system sale to Kuwait and in the Patriot air and missile defence replenishment sale to Qatar, the State Department added.
Northrop Grumman was also a principal contractor in the Kuwaiti sale.
Over the years, Washington has faced scrutiny for military ties with Kuwait, the UAE and Qatar over those countries' human rights track records that rights advocates say involve restrictions on and reported abuses of minorities, journalists, voices of dissent, the LGBT community and labourers.
Those nations have denied supporting or engaging in domestic rights abuses.
US support for Israel has also come under scrutiny from rights experts, particularly over Israel's assault on Gaza that has killed tens of thousands, caused a hunger crisis and led to assessments of genocide from scholars and a UN inquiry.
Israel calls its actions self-defence after Hamas-led group killed 1,200 people in an October 2023 attack.
Washington has maintained support for its allies.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Give us our privacy,' says family of teen stabbed 61 times
Article:
KUALA KANGSAR: The family of a 19-year-old teenager who was stabbed 61 times on Thursday night has appealed for privacy as they struggle to come to terms with the tragedy.
The victim's uncle, who declined to be named, said the family was still in shock and not in a position to speak further about the incident.
"We really can't say anything right now. We're still trying to process everything that has happened," he said when met at their home in Kampung Batu Jarau here today.
Close family members and relatives are still gathered with the family.
The body of Nurfisya Zulkifly, a student of Kolej Poly-Tech MARA, was laid to rest at the Kampung Periang Muslim Cemetery in Sauk here at 5.30am.
The funeral prayer was also held at the Kampung Periang Mosque.
He added that the family had only just returned from Kelantan and were physically and emotionally drained.
"We haven't slept, we haven't eaten, and we're extremely exhausted from the journey. Please give us some space," he said.
He added that the family was not refusing to cooperate with the media, but needed time to regain their composure.
"It's not that we don't want to cooperate with journalists, but we are afraid we might say things we shouldn't in this situation. We hope everyone can understand and allow us time to calm down first," he said.
It was reported that the victim was found dead with 61 stab wounds and left by the roadside in Kampung Simah, Ketereh, Kelantan on Thursday night.
She was found by villagers at 12.30am, lying in a pool of blood with injuries from the face to the legs, mostly concentrated on the right side of the body.
Following this, police arrested a 60-year-old woman and three men over the murder of the student.
Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said the suspects, aged between 19 and 66, were detained in two separate raids at Taman Murni and Kampung Belukar Luas between 4.40pm yesterday and 2.30am today.
Police also seized two cars, two mobile phones, clothing, a pair of slippers, four floor mats, and a knife believed to be the murder weapon.
A post-mortem confirmed the
cause of death
as multiple stab wounds to the chest.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Many want to pit me against Zahid, but I remain loyal,' says Tok Mat [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno deputy president Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan has brushed aside attempts to drive a wedge between him and party president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, declaring his loyalty to the party leadership amid growing speculation of internal rifts.
Speaking at Umno's 80th anniversary celebration at the World Trade Centre here, Mohamad said there had been persistent efforts by certain quarters to create friction between him and Zahid.
"Many want to pit us against each other, especially to pit me against Datuk Seri. Not a few… many," he said.
"But I choose to remain loyal to the party and support Datuk Seri."
Mohamad, who is also known as Tok Mat, said he had deliberately ignored such narratives and remained focused on strengthening Umno from within.
"People say all sorts of things, but I turn a deaf ear, close my eyes, and keep my heart and soul committed to the party we uphold," he added.
Mohamad added that unity and loyalty had been the cornerstone of Umno's survival over the past eight decades.
"What has saved us is unity, Datuk Seri. What has saved us is unity and loyalty to the party. There is no substitute for this," he said.
He added that divisions within the party would leave it vulnerable to manipulation by vested interests, both internally and externally.
"A divided party will be easily used and manipulated by those with interests, from within and outside. It will gradually sideline and diminish our role as Umno," he said.
Mohamad also cautioned against elements attempting to sow discord among party members, particularly at a critical juncture.
"At a time as important as this, there must be no elements trying to incite division and pit us against one another," he said.
Reflecting on Umno's journey, Mohamad acknowledged the party was now operating in a vastly different environment shaped by digitalisation, and called for careful strategic planning moving forward.
"We must organise our steps properly because Umno is now operating in a world that has changed from what we once knew… the world of digitalisation," he said.
Marking his 70th birthday, Mohamad said his motivations were no longer tied to personal ambition, but to contributing back to the party and the country.
"I have nothing to lose. I'm not talking about career anymore, but I want to give back to my party, I want to give back to my country," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Stop selling history, start selling the future,' Tok Mat tells Umno on 80th anniversary
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno deputy president Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan has told the party to stop harping on past glories and start offering the Malay community a credible vision for the future.
"Stop selling history. Start selling the future," Mohamad said in his opening speech at Umno's 80th anniversary celebrations at World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur.
He said emotional appeals, sympathy, or reminders of past debts of gratitude no longer moved the community.
What Malays want, he said, was confidence in a future that only Umno could offer.
"The reality is that the Malay community today is no longer as close to Umno as it once was. In many respects, Umno appears to have been sidelined from the narrative of their success," he said.
Mohamad, commonly known as Tok Mat, did not shy away from revisiting the party's lowest point in 2018, when Umno fell from power for the first time.
He said the party had been offered eulogies and condolences, and had come close to being written off by its opponents and some within its own ranks.
"But Umno endured. It was not buried," he said, adding that every storm the party had weathered was navigated with an unshakeable resolve to recover.
In his speech, he outlined five achievements since 2018 - party stability, continued participation in government, credible ministerial performance, a push towards inclusive consolidation under the Rumah Bangsa concept, and steadfastness of principle under pressure.
Mohamad also spoke about the road ahead, laying out five challenges the party must confront.
These include elevating Malay competitiveness in a global and digital economy, managing relations with non-Malay communities and the Bumiputera of Sabah and Sarawak, and preparing the next generation of leadership.
"Umno must restore the confidence of the people through performance, integrity, and tangible reform," he said.
Mohamad questioned whether the party had done enough to nurture sophisticated leaders to manage a deeply cosmopolitan Malaysia.
He said the party must be ready to field younger, more innovative leaders.
"If we do nothing from this moment, the influence, strength, and visibility of Umno will suffer in the long run," he warned.
He called on Umno to return to its founding ideology as a reform movement rooted in Islam, saying the faith must serve as the inspiration for the party's actions.
"I cannot imagine Malays without Islam. Malays without Umno, that is familiar enough. But Malays without Islam, that would be a deviation from the ordinary," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysian haj pilgrim dies after collapsing at Makkah Grand Mosque
Article:
MAKKAH: A Malaysian haj pilgrim died after collapsing while on his way to perform Friday prayers at the courtyard of the Grand Mosque here yesterday.
Malaysian Haj Delegation head Mohd Hisham Harun said the 65-year-old male pilgrim from Pahang was pronounced dead at 12.15pm local time due to heart complications.
Mohd Hisham said the deceased, who had travelled to the Holy Land with his wife, was at the Grand Mosque courtyard to perform Friday prayers when he suddenly collapsed and lost consciousness.
"Initial treatment, including resuscitation, was administered at the scene before he was taken to Jiwar Jabal Omar Hospital for further treatment.
"However, the pilgrim was pronounced dead at the medical centre," he said at a press conference here today.
He said all funeral arrangements for the deceased were fully managed by Tabung Haji including bringing the body to the Grand Mosque for funeral prayers after Maghrib before being laid to rest at the Al Sharayea Cemetery here.
He added that welfare unit personnel, counsellors and haj religious guides had also visited the deceased's wife to provide support and necessary assistance.
"Tabung Haji will also perform badal haj on behalf of the deceased as well as organise a tahlil recital ceremony with other pilgrims," he said. – BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Uni student stabbed 61 times: 2 youths remanded for 7 days [WATCH]
Article:
KOTA BHARU: Two youths, suspects in the murder of a university student who was stabbed 61 times, have been remanded for seven days.
Two other suspects, a 60-year-old woman and a 66-year-old man, were remanded for three days.
The order was issued by Magistrate Amirul Asyraf Abdul Rasid today.
The case is being investigated under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder, which carries the death penalty or imprisonment of between 30 and 40 years, and whipping upon conviction.
Earlier, Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said the four suspects were
arrested
in raids at two separate locations in Taman Murni and Kampung Belukar Luas, Ketereh, between 4pm yesterday and early this morning.
The suspects have no prior criminal records and tested negative for drugs.
It was previously reported that the victim's body was
found by the roadside
in Kampung Simah, Ketereh, on Friday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Umno sees nearly 14,000 new members join in under 2 weeks [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno has recorded 13,954 new memberships since opening its online registration portal on April 20, secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said.
Speaking at Umno's 80th anniversary celebrations here today, Asyraf said the figure, accumulated in less than two weeks, reflected the growing appeal of a simplified membership process introduced under party president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
Youth members led the surge, recording 8,233 new sign-ups, followed by Puteri with 1,260 and Wanita with 1,240.
Johor topped the list of states for new memberships, followed by Pahang and Perak.
"I can say that this number increases almost every day," Asyraf said in his welcoming speech at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur.
Asyraf said the strong response was due to a policy shift championed by Zahid at the Supreme Council, which removed barriers for young Malaysians joining the party.
New members are now recognised as full members on equal standing with ordinary members, rather than as associate members.
"This is a game changer introduced by the president, who truly understands the hearts of young Malaysians," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gaza activists disperse after flotilla halted by Israel off Crete
Article:
ATHENS, (Greece): Dozens of activists on a Gaza-bound aid flotilla intercepted by Israeli forces off Crete left their boats on Friday, many taken to hospital on the Greek island while others were flown out.
Greek officials said 31 of the roughly 175 activists had been taken to hospital on Crete.
Turkish officials said about 60 had arrived by plane in Istanbul on Friday evening.
More than 50 vessels had set sail from ports in France, Spain and Italy with the aim of breaking the blockade of Gaza and bringing humanitarian aid to the Palestinian territory.
Israeli forces halted the boats overnight Wednesday to Thursday, with organisers the Global Sumud Flotilla saying their equipment had been smashed and that the intervention had left them facing a "calculated death trap at sea."
As they approached the port on Friday, the activists chanted "Free Palestine", an AFP reporter saw.
Israel controls all entry points to Gaza. It has been accused by the United Nations and foreign NGOs of strangling the flow of goods into the territory, causing shortages since the start of Israel's war against the Palestinian militant group Hamas in Oct 2023.
The Gaza Strip, governed by Hamas, has been under an Israeli blockade since 2007.
- 'Provocation' -
Israel's foreign ministry earlier said around 175 activists had been taken off more than 20 vessels on Thursday by its country's military. Flotilla organisers put the number of "kidnapped" activists at 211.
Israeli foreign ministry spokesman Oren Marmorstein on Friday said: "All the flotilla activists are now in Greece except Saif Abu Keshek and Thiago Avila."
In a post on X, the ministry called the flotilla "another provocation designed to divert attention from Hamas's refusal to disarm."
It also said the flotilla was serving "professional provocateurs."
It said Keshek was "suspected of affiliation with a terrorist organisation" and that he and Avila, suspected of "illegal activity", would be brought to Israel for questioning.
Several European governments with nationals among those arrested have called on Israel to free the activists and called its action a flagrant contravention of international law.
Spain demanded the immediate release of Keshek, a Spanish national.
Israel's closest ally, the United States, said the flotilla was a "stunt" and the vessels should be denied port access, docking, departure and refuelling.
A State Department spokesman said Washington was exploring imposing "consequences" on those who support the flotilla.
The war in Gaza, triggered by the Hamas attacks on Israel on Oct 7, 2023, has led to severe shortages of food, water, medicine and fuel.
In the summer and autumn of 2025, a first voyage by the Global Sumud Flotilla across the Mediterranean towards Gaza drew worldwide attention.
The boats in that flotilla were intercepted by Israel off the coasts of Egypt and the Gaza Strip in early October.
Crew members, including Swedish activist Greta Thunberg, were arrested and then expelled by Israel. — AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US approves possible sale of Advanced Precision Kill Weapon System to Israel
Article:
WASHINGTON: The US State Department said Friday that it approved the possible sale of Advanced Precision Kill Weapon System (APKWS) and related equipment to Israel, Anadolu Ajansi reported.
The estimated total cost is US$992.4 million (RM3.9 billion), it said in a statement.
"The proposed sale will improve Israel's capability to meet current and future threats, strengthen its homeland defence, and serve as a deterrent to regional threats. Israel will have no difficulty absorbing these articles and services into its armed forces," it added.
The principal contractor will be BAE Systems in the US state of New Hampshire.
The US has long been criticised for providing support to Israel for its genocideal war in the Gaza Strip, where more than 72,000 people, mostly women and children, have been killed since October 2023.
A ceasefire was reached in October 2025, but violations have been frequent.
Several American lawmakers have demanded that the US stop supplying weapons to Israel and accused it of being complicit in Israel's war in Gaza.
Meanwhile, the US Department of State also approved the possible sale of Patriot Air and Missile Defence replenishment services and related equipment to Qatar for US$4.01 billion.
It also approved the potential sale of APKWS to Qatar for US$992 million, and to the UAE for US$147.6 million.
Separately, it green-lighted the possible sale of Integrated Battle Command System to Kuwait for US$2.5 billion. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 10,300 litres of diesel seized in Johor illegal trading bust
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Johor Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry has busted an illegal diesel trading syndicate following a raid on an industrial premises in Ulu Tiram here on Thursday.
The ministry's Johor director, Lilis Saslinda Pornomo, said the raid was carried out by its enforcement division under Op Tiris 4.0.
Checks found the premises, believed to be involved in diesel trading and wholesale, had been operating without a valid licence.
"The syndicate is believed to have been operating under the guise of a diesel wholesaler.
"They obtained supplies from a supplier company before reselling them to transport companies without a valid licence," she said in a statement today.
Lilis Saslinda said enforcement officers seized an estimated 10,300 litres of diesel, a skid tank, and a fuel pump set complete with nozzle and hose.
Several transaction-related documents were also seized, bringing the total value of the haul to an estimated RM85,736.
A local man in his 60s was also detained to assist in investigations.
She said the case is being investigated under the Supply Control Act 1961.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cops hunt Myvi driver who struck motorcyclist with a stick
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Police are looking for a man who allegedly attacked a motorcyclist with a wooden stick after a video of the incident went viral.
Johor Baru South police chief Assistant Commissioner Raub Selamat said the 30-second video shows a Perodua Myvi driver acting aggressively and provoking a motorcyclist before hitting him in the middle of the road.
"The incident is believed to have occurred at a petrol station in Jalan Bukit Chagar, Bukit Senyum.
"No police reports have been lodged by any of the parties involved so far," Raub said in a statement.
He added that police are working to identify those involved and determine the motive.
Those with information are urged to contact the Johor Baru South police headquarters hotline at 07-2182323.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trust, transparency and the architecture of zakat governance
Article:
Samirul Ariff Othman
MALAYSIA's zakat collections have grown steadily over the past decade, reflecting both rising incomes and stronger institutional reach.
In several states, annual collections now exceed RM1 billion, with total nationwide zakat contributions estimated at over RM4 billion-RM5 billion annually.
This scale underscores the increasing economic significance of zakat as a redistributive mechanism within the country's Islamic financial ecosystem.
Zakat is not merely a charitable act. It is a foundational pillar of the Islamic economic framework, built on faith, redistribution, and social responsibility.
Properly administered, it plays a stabilising role - cushioning vulnerable households, supporting micro-enterprises, and complementing state welfare systems.
Yet as zakat systems expand in scale and complexity, a fundamental question arises: can traditional trust-based frameworks keep pace with modern financial realities?
Recent developments across jurisdictions suggest a deeper structural issue.
The challenge is not simply one of compliance or misconduct. It is one of system design - how institutions are structured to manage large, trust-based financial flows in an increasingly complex environment.
Three Models, Three Trade-Offs
A comparative view of zakat governance across Malaysia, Indonesia and the Gulf highlights distinct institutional approaches, each with its own trade-offs.
Malaysia operates a decentralised, state-based system.
Zakat administration falls under state Islamic religious councils (MAIN), with each state managing its own collection and distribution.
This allows flexibility and alignment with local socio-economic conditions - a critical advantage in a federal system with varying development profiles.
However, decentralisation also introduces fragmentation.
Governance standards vary across states, coordination remains limited, and data integration is uneven.
More importantly, a parallel ecosystem of non-state actors including NGOs and private charitable initiatives operates alongside formal institutions.
While many perform commendable work, their position outside a unified regulatory framework creates potential gaps in oversight.
Indonesia presents a more integrated model.
The National Zakat Agency (BAZNAS) coordinates at the centre, while private zakat institutions (LAZ) operate under licensing, reporting, and audit requirements.
Indonesia's zakat collection has also expanded significantly, reaching tens of trillions of rupiah annually, supported by digital platforms and nationwide campaigns.
This hybrid structure brings non-state actors into the formal system, ensuring that plural participation does not come at the expense of accountability.
Compliance challenges remain, particularly across Indonesia's vast geography, but transparency is structurally embedded.
In contrast, Gulf countries tend toward centralisation. Zakat is either state-managed or tightly supervised by religious authorities.
This reduces the space for unregulated actors and ensures consistency in governance.
The trade-off, however, lies in reduced flexibility and, in some cases, varying degrees of public transparency.
The Core Issue: Architecture, Not Intent
Across these systems, one lesson is clear: effectiveness is determined not by intent, but by architecture.
Where authority is fragmented, risks accumulate quietly. Where participation is broad but weakly regulated, transparency may lag behind scale.
Where control is centralised, accountability must be actively maintained to preserve public confidence.
For Malaysia, the issue is not whether the system functions - it does.
Zakat institutions have played an important role in poverty alleviation and social support. The question is whether the current architecture is sufficiently robust for the scale it has reached.
From Fragmentation To Integration
Several policy directions emerge from this comparative analysis.
First, stronger coordination across state-level institutions could improve consistency without undermining Malaysia's federal structure.
Shared benchmarks, integrated reporting systems, and cross-state data platforms would reduce fragmentation and improve oversight.
Second, non-state actors should be progressively integrated into a formal regulatory framework.
Licensing, reporting obligations, and periodic audits can ensure that NGOs operate within a transparent ecosystem, rather than parallel to it. This would align Malaysia more closely with Indonesia's hybrid model.
Third, digitalisation offers a powerful lever. Real-time reporting, integrated databases, and public dashboards can enhance visibility across the system.
In an era where financial technology is transforming other sectors, zakat governance cannot remain analogue.
Finally, enforcement capacity must evolve alongside scale.
As financial flows increase into the billions, monitoring and investigative capabilities must keep pace. Risk management in such systems cannot rely solely on goodwill; it must be institutionalised.
Trust Must Be Reinforced
Zakat is built on trust. But in complex systems, trust cannot be assumed - it must be reinforced through structure.
Good intentions are no longer sufficient. Integrity must be verifiable. This is not a question of doubt, but of stewardship.
Institutions entrusted with zakat manage not only financial resources, but public confidence. Once eroded, that confidence is difficult to rebuild.
Conclusion: Sustaining Confidence In A Growing System
As societies modernise, institutions must adapt. The balance between trust and accountability, flexibility and control, must be recalibrated continuously.
For Malaysia, the path forward is not to replace its model, but to strengthen it - drawing from comparative experiences while addressing structural gaps.
The objective is not centralisation for its own sake, but coherence, transparency and resilience.
In the end, the measure of a zakat system is not how much it collects, but how well it sustains trust.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Reliance on small group of tech vendors seen risky amid hacking attempts
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Recent hacking attempts targeting stock trading accounts have raised concerns about weakenesses in the country's capital market system.
While investigations of recent cases are still ongoing, one of the most critical risks is the heavy reliance on a small number of technology vendors, said AKATI Sekurity chief executive officer Krishna Rajapgopal.
"The concentration creates a situation where a single point of failure can affect multiple institutions simultaneously, amplifying the impact of any breach," he told Business Times.
Much of the market depends on software provided by only a few vendors, meaning that if one system is compromised, several brokerage firms can be affected at the same time, he added.
The interconnected structure allows attackers to target shared infrastructure rather than individual firms, potentially disrupting a significant portion of the market in a short period.
Similar patterns, Rajagopal added, have been observed internationally, where threat actors exploit systemic dependencies to maximise impact.
The nature of these attacks also differs from more common banking scams as they often require a deeper understanding of how trading systems operate.
"Many of the attackers involved possess prior knowledge of brokerage environments or related software, enabling them to identify and exploit specific weaknesses more effectively.
"This level of sophistication increases the risk of unauthorised trading activity, including the potential for rapid liquidation of assets before detection," he added.
Attempts to compromise trading accounts have surfaced multiple times since late 2024, with recurring incidents suggesting that underlying vulnerabilities may not have been fully addressed.
For investors, these risks can be significant, particularly when disputes arise over responsibility for unauthorised transactions.
In many cases, affected individuals may find themselves caught between brokerage firms and system providers, with limited clarity on where accountability lies.
Rajagopal stressed that cybersecurity risks in the capital market are now comparable to those seen in traditional banking scams, describing them as an equally viable target for cybercriminals.
He added that the difference lies mainly in the level of expertise required, as trading-related attacks tend to involve more technically knowledgeable actors.
Addressing these challenges, he said, will require coordinated action across the industry, including regulators, financial institutions, and technology providers.
Without stronger oversight, improved accountability, and a reduction in concentration risk, systemic vulnerabilities are likely to persist.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: After withdrawing support, Negeri Sembilan Umno says MB’s fate now with BN, PH leadership
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Negeri Sembilan Umno will defer to party leadership on its next course of action following its withdrawal of support for the menteri besar, including any decision on a potential replacement, its chief Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias said today.
Jalaluddin said the state chapter would abide by decisions made by Barisan Nasional (BN) and Pakatan Harapan (PH) leadership, including whether the next menteri besar should come from outside Umno.
“We will wait and see what the leadership decides, be it BN or PH. It is not for Negeri Sembilan Umno to determine,” he told reporters when met outside Merdeka Hall at the World Trade Centre.
He stressed that the current political crisis was “extraordinary”, following the decision by 14 Umno assemblymen on April 27 to withdraw support for the menteri besar.
Jalaluddin said the move came after issues the group could no longer accept, adding that the crisis was triggered by what he described as mismanagement by the menteri besar that disrupted the state’s political harmony.
“This is not about politics, power or ‘tebuk atap’ (back door). This concerns governance and the institutions of adat and the Negeri Sembilan monarchy,” he said.
He added that the loss of confidence by the 14 assemblymen was final and not a shifting political stance.
“If we have pulled support and the menteri besar continues to govern, it means we have lost trust,” he said.
Jalaluddin reiterated that Umno Negeri Sembilan is prepared to take on the role of opposition if directed by party leadership, potentially until the next general election.
“What we will do next depends on Umno’s decision. We don’t mind being in the opposition and if we are to be in the opposition, we will remain so until GE16,” he said.
On speculation over a replacement menteri besar, including the possibility of a PKR candidate, he said the matter was premature.
He warned that public debate surrounding the issue risked creating confusion, particularly among younger Malaysians, and could harm the reputation of Negeri Sembilan’s adat institutions.
“The question now is not about individuals or positions, but about the responsibility of a menteri besar. We cannot allow a crisis like this to happen, especially one involving adat,” he said.
“When such matters are debated openly, many will not fully understand adat. This can lead to negative perceptions and confusion among youths and students,” he added.
Jalaluddin said Umno was compelled to act to prevent further damage.
Earlier in his speech, Jalaluddin said many had accused Umno of betraying the government, but they forget that in 2023, Umno Negeri Sembilan won 14 seats — the most among all parties.
In the 2023 Negeri Sembilan state polls, Umno secured 14 seats, followed by PKR (five), DAP (11), Perikatan Nasional (five), and Amanah (one).
Jalaluddin pointed out that with the strongest representation at the time, he could have moved to destabilise the government earlier if he had intended to do so.
“If I wanted to stir something, I could have done it then but I respected the federal leadership’s call to maintain peace and stability so that we could work together,” he said.
He said Umno had continued to play its role within the state government over the past two-and-a-half years, including holding four executive council (exco) positions.
Rejecting accusations of a “tebuk atap” (backdoor takeover), Jalaluddin said such claims were misplaced.
“Tebuk atap is when you try to take over the government from outside. We are already part of the government — we have exco positions and allocations. What ‘tebuk atap’ is this?” he asked.
He stressed that the withdrawal of support was prompted by dissatisfaction with the state government’s handling of a recent crisis, which he said could not be compromised.
“We are also close to the general election, but we cannot compromise when there is failure in governance,” he said.
Jalaluddin said Umno Negeri Sembilan would now sit in the opposition for as long as it believes the state government is not performing effectively.
He added that the party would focus on strengthening its position ahead of the next general election.
“We will do our best to win all seats. We do not need to cater to others’ feelings. As chairman of the state Umno machinery, it is my responsibility to ensure we are in a dominant position,” he said.
He also rejected claims that Umno’s actions were motivated by a desire for power or positions.
“Umno Negeri Sembilan is not greedy or hungry for posts. We did not want to disrupt the momentum, but we acted because we have principles we cannot abandon — integrity, and our promises to religion and nation,” he said.
Reaffirming the party’s stance, Jalaluddin ended with a firm message: “No retreat, no surrender.”

Title: Jealous rage likely motive behind Ketereh college girl’s killing, Kelantan police say amid week-long remand for boyfriend and buddy
Article:
KOTA BHARU, May 2 — Jealousy is believed to be the motive behind the murder of a 19-year-old college student whose body was found with 61 stab wounds in Keterah yesterday.
Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said investigators suspect the victim was stabbed to death by her 19-year-old boyfriend, who allegedly believed she was having an affair, the
New Straits Times
reported today.
“Initial investigations showed that an argument broke out between the victim and the suspect, which led to her death.
“She is believed to have been stabbed while inside the suspect’s car,” the senior policeman was quoted as saying.
He said the boyfriend, identified as the main suspect, was arrested yesterday alongside another youth believed to be a friend of the victim.
Both are now under a seven-day remand order.
The policeman added that the boyfriend’s parents — a 66-year-old man and a 60-year-old woman – have also been detained for the next three days to assist in investigations.
The remand orders were issued by Magistrate Amirul Asyraf Abdul Rasid today, the
New Straits Times
reported.
Mohd Yusoff confirmed all suspects have no prior criminal records and tested negative for drugs.
He said investigations into the case are ongoing under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder, which carry the death penalty or imprisonment of between 30 and 40 years, as well as whipping upon conviction.
Local media had earlier identified the victim as 19-year-old Nurfisya Zulkifly from Kuala Kangsar, Perak who had just returned to her Ketereh college campus to start a new semester a week ago.
Her body with 61 stab wounds was found by the roadside near a paddy field in Kampung Simah yesterday and was buried early this morning in her hometown.

Title: One day, two fatal falls at KLIA2: Police identify second foreigner as 30-year-old Algerian man
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, May 2 — Police have confirmed another fatal incident of a foreigner after falling at Kuala Lumpur International Airport Terminal 2 (KLIA T2), last night.
KLIA district police chief ACP M. Ravi said in the incident at about 9.10 pm, a 30-year-old Algerian man is believed to have fallen from the outside of the balcony at the terminal.
He said the medical team provided emergency treatment at the scene before the victim was taken to the Cyberjaya Hospital for further treatment.
“However, at about 2.55 am this morning, the hospital confirmed that the man had died,” he said in a statement today.
The case is classified as a Sudden Death Report (SDR) and the cause of the incident is still being investigated
Anyone with information is asked to contact the KLIA district police headquarters at 03-8776 8222 to assist in the investigation.
Yesterday afternoon, police reported that a woman from China fell to her death from Level 3 of Kuala Lumpur International Airport Terminal 2. — Bernama

Title: Regular play spot but not her slippers, says mum of missing nine-year-old girl swept into Bandar Mahkota Cheras drain
Article:
KAJANG, May 2 — A nine-year-old girl, feared drowned after falling into a drain and swept away by strong currents in Bandar Mahkota Cheras yesterday, often played in the area where the incident occurred.
Her mother, Nordina Zulkarnain, 34, said the open area next to the apartment was a popular spot for local children to play in, including her daughter Nur Qaseh Deandra Mohd Qayyum.
“She was playing there yesterday because it was a public holiday, hanging out with her friends from the apartment.
“After it happened, my second son, her brother, rushed home and told my husband and me,” she told reporters at the scene here today.
Nordina said her husband then immediately called emergency services for fire department assistance.
On the slippers and other items found in today’s search area, she confirmed they were not her daughter’s.
Day Two of a search and rescue for a nine-year-old girl who was swept away by strong currents in Bandar Mahkota Cheras, Selangor on May 1, 2026. — Bernama pic
Meanwhile, the victim’s grandfather, Zulkarnain Ahmad, 57, who rushed from Seremban to the scene yesterday, said he could not sleep at all since yesterday.
“Today I’m joining the rescue teams searching the area,” he said.
A search and rescue for a nine-year-old girl who was swept away by strong currents in Bandar Mahkota Cheras, Selangor on May 1, 2026, expands into the underground drainage system on Day Two.— Bernama pic
Media reports yesterday cited that a nine-year-old girl fell into a drain while chasing a ball, before being swept away by strong currents.
Firefighters received the emergency call about the incident at Sri Hijau Condominium at 7.08 pm. — Bernama

Title: DBKL imposes 300m buffer zone on Bukit Kiara project after TTDI residents’ concerns, says Hannah Yeoh
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) has imposed a 300m buffer zone on a proposed development opposite the Taman Persekutuan Bukit Kiara recreational park, following complaints from residents in Taman Tun Dr Ismail (TTDI).
Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said the decision was made after taking into account the potential impact on nearby residents and local biodiversity,
The Star
reported.
“I am very aware of how important parks are, not just for those living in Bukit Kiara, but for everyone in Kuala Lumpur.
“Kuala Lumpur is a city, and its people need green spaces for recreational activities and to de-stress,” she said.
Yeoh spoke to the media after an event at the park today, organised by local non-profit Friends of Bukit Dinding (FoBK), where Kuala Lumpur mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud and FoBK president Leon Koay were also present.
She told
The Star
that no development order had been issued to the developer and that negotiations were still ongoing.
The announcement follows protests by TTDI residents against a proposed pair of 30-storey condominiums next to the park.
The project, on a 3.42ha site, is undergoing an environmental impact assessment as part of the approval process, the English daily reported.
Residents had raised concerns that the development would disrupt the park’s natural ecosystem and worsen traffic along Changkat Abang Haji Openg, Jalan Tun Mohd Fuad, and Jalan Datuk Sulaiman.
On April 12, residents held a town hall session to launch a petition calling for the buffer zone to be increased to between 300m and 500m, compared with a previous 50m guideline set by the National Landscape Department (JLN), which manages the park.
Last August, Friends of Bukit Dinding (FoBK) filed a judicial review seeking a stay order to stop City Hall from granting approvals for the project, among other requests.
The Kuala Lumpur High Court granted leave in September.

Title: Funeral in Kuala Kangsar for murdered college student, 19; grieving family asks for privacy
Article:
KUALA KANGSAR, May 2 — The family of a 19-year-old student found stabbed to death 61 times in Kampung Simah, Ketereh, Kelantan, asks for space to grieve in peace.
A visit to the victim’s family home in Kampung Batu Jarau here revealed a solemn silence, with only her closest relatives present.
“We’d like some privacy. We need some quiet because we’re exhausted and haven’t slept since yesterday.
“When we’re ready, we’ll talk to the media,” said the victim’s uncle, who didn’t want to be named.
The remains of Nurfisya Zulkifly were laid to rest at 5.30 am today at Kampung Periang Muslim Cemetery here, following a post-mortem at the Forensic Unit of Raja Perempuan Zainab II Hospital in Kota Bharu last night.
Yesterday, the victim’s brother, Mohd Faris Zulkifly, 21, said the family came to Kelantan last Saturday to drop off Nurfisya for her new semester, only to be informed about the tragic news at noon.
Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said that the victim was believed to have been murdered elsewhere before her body was dumped near the padi fields in the village, adding that the case is being probed under Section 302 of the Penal Code.
Four suspects, three men aged 19 to 66 and a 60-year-old woman, were remanded this morning to assist with the investigation. — Bernama

Title: Second-day search underway for nine-year-old feared swept away in Bandar Mahkota Cheras drain
Article:
KAJANG, May 2 — The search and rescue (SAR) operation for a nine-year-old girl feared swept away after falling into a drain in Bandar Mahkota Cheras while retrieving a ball has entered its second day, with search efforts expanded to a five-kilometre radius.
According to
Sinar Harian
, Selangor Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) operations assistant director Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the operation involves firefighters from the Kajang station, supported by two Water Rescue Team (PPDA) units from Shah Alam and KLIA.
He said other agencies assisting in the operation include the K9 Unit, Indah Water Konsortium (IWK), the Drainage and Irrigation Department (JPS), Rescue Bencana Malaysia, the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM), and the Civil Defence Force (APM).
“The PPDA team will trace the sewer system from the location where the victim fell towards Sungai Long,” he said at a press conference at the scene today, as reported by
Sinar Harian
.
Ahmad Mukhlis said the search team is also using IWK’s pole camera to assist in scanning inside the sewer system.
He added that rescue personnel will also enter the sewer system to search for the victim, covering a distance of about 270 metres.
“The sewer is estimated to be about 50 metres below ground level, with a diameter of around two metres,” he was quoted as saying.
He said the department received an emergency call regarding the incident at about 7.08 pm yesterday.
It is understood that the girl was playing with a ball with friends near her residential area when the ball fell into the drain.
Ahmad Mukhlis said four children were initially playing when the ball rolled into the drain.
He said the victim reportedly tried to retrieve the ball but was swept away by strong currents.
Her friends attempted to hold her back, but were unable to do so due to slippery conditions, rain, and strong water flow, he added.

Title: Umno must reinvent itself or risk irrelevance, says Mohamad Hasan as party marks 80th anniversary
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Umno deputy president Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan today called for a sweeping reinvention of the party, warning that it risks being left behind if it fails to adapt to shifting voter sentiment and reconnect with Malays who are increasingly drifting away.
Speaking at Umno’s 80th anniversary gathering — which coincided with his 70th birthday — the Rembau MP said the party could no longer rely on legacy or nostalgia to stay relevant.
“Reality is, Malays are not as close to us as before. Some even say they no longer need Umno,” he said.
“However, Umno is not dead. Despite all the trials and tribulations, Umno is being tested and whenever we are tested, we come back stronger,” he said in his opening speech at the convention at the World Trade Centre.
Mohamad acknowledged that the party’s loss of federal power in 2018 had eroded public trust, but said Umno has since drawn strength from the crisis and grassroots loyalty.
Framing the party’s future around adaptability, he stressed that Umno must move away from rigid political formulas and adopt a more flexible, data-driven approach ahead of state elections and the next general election.
“Elections today are no longer static. This is not weakness — flexibility is our strength,” he said, adding that modern campaigns are now a “war of data” rather than traditional ceramah-driven contests.
He cautioned that internal divisions would undermine any chance of a political comeback.
“If we fight among ourselves before the general election, we will lose,” he warned.
He also urged the party to undertake a deeper ideological reset, saying Umno must better define its direction in the face of digitalisation and globalisation, particularly as younger, digitally native voters grow up in a borderless political environment.
At the same time, Mohamad said Umno must balance its role as a political vehicle for Malays and Islam with the realities of Malaysia’s multicultural society.
